targets.name: NPM Cache
targets.consequence: Removes the local NPM package cache. The next time you run "npm install" in a project, packages will be re-downloaded from the internet, so the first install may take a bit longer.

targets.name: Pip Cache
targets.consequence: Clears Python package caches. Packages will be re-downloaded from PyPI on the next "pip install". This does not harm your system.

targets.name: Yarn Cache
targets.consequence: Clears Yarn package caches. The next "yarn install" will re-download packages from the internet, so the first install may take slightly longer.

targets.name: Cargo Registry
targets.description: Rust package registry and build caches
targets.consequence: Clears the Rust Cargo registry cache. Libraries will be re-downloaded on the next Rust project build, so the first compile may take a bit longer.

targets.name: Xcode Derived Data
targets.description: Xcode build outputs and index data
targets.consequence: Deletes Xcode build outputs and indexing data. Your source code is not deleted. The next build will compile from scratch, which takes longer, but this fixes many Xcode build issues.

targets.name: Application Caches
targets.consequence: Deletes local caches for apps like Chrome and Spotify. Apps will rebuild these caches on launch. It is recommended to restart your computer after cleaning them.

targets.name: Application Logs
targets.consequence: Deletes historical application log files. This does not affect system operation, but you will lose past logs that could be used for troubleshooting.

targets.name: Trash
targets.consequence: Permanently deletes files in the Trash to free space immediately. This action cannot be undone.
